key_insight: An automated seam-welding system with controlled rotation and pneumatic fixturing can produce more uniform pipe welds while sharply reducing leakage failures.
engineering_relevance: The work shows how low-cost mechanical automation and thermal management can improve throughput, safety, and repeatability in pipe joining applications.
method: Built a rigid frame with specialized pipe-holding fixtures and programmable pneumatic clamps.
Used a gear-motor drive to maintain 10–15 RPM during continuous seam welding.
Integrated internal water-cooled CuCrZr rotating wheel electrodes to limit heat buildup.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports comparative testing on CRCS and ER34 pipes with 11–13 A welding current and 2.5 mm wall thickness, reducing leakage failure from about 15% to below 3%.
watch_item: Performance is reported for a narrow set of materials and process settings, so broader validation across pipe sizes, alloys, and duty cycles is still needed.
paper_title: Design and Development of Automatic Seam Welding of Pipes
